title: The Delta Where Roars Lose Their Difference

features.name: radio-transmission-chip
features.desc-on-touch: a PCB where stations' signals accidentally synchronized. individual broadcasts dissolved into one undifferentiated roar. the merger is permanent.

features.name: coastal-antenna-tower
features.desc-on-touch: copper has oxidized from listening. the antenna no longer knows which roar belongs to which voice. it has learned to hear only the merged sound.

features.name: signal-merger-pool
features.desc-on-touch: where individual waves collapse into one undifferentiated ocean-voice. separation becomes impossible. the roar forgets how to be multiple.

features.name: decommissioned-sonar-frame
features.desc-on-touch: equipment that plotted sound as if sound could be plotted. now it just receives. now it just listens to things that have forgotten their own echoes.

features.name: confluence-marker-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: lines converging but no longer resolving. the marker is watching the roars collapse into one voice. the glyph is learning silence.

inscription: where all roars collapse into one ocean-voice that has forgotten how to be separate

file.name: CoastalRadioDepot_SonarLogs_MergedSignals_1960s-1987.wav
file.note: recordings where individual stations accidentally synchronized; broadcast cacophonies dissolved into singular ocean roar; separation became impossible.

exits.label: back to where roars still remember being speech
toward waters where even silence has become a frequency
deeper delta where the roar learns to listen to itself
into the listening chamber where the irreparable seam grows wider
